ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'salad food product' class (FOODON_00002473); no specific code exists for a customisable 4-topping undressed salad. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers the broad 'Salads' category. No specific code for a 4-topping undressed variant exists; closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for this customisable salad preparation. Null retained to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: choice of four toppings (e.g. hard-boiled egg, tuna, corn, croutons, nuts, seeds)
allergens: potential allergens vary by topping selection (e.g. egg, fish, gluten from croutons, tree nuts)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 200 g (one bowl or plate), representing a typical single-portion salad with four toppings and no sauce as served in Singapore food establishments.

nutrient_source.title: Salad (4 toppings), no sauce — HPB Singapore Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Salad (4 toppings), no sauce' (cr_id F08010044). All per-100g values are sourced at confidence 0.95 as recorded in the input metadata.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed salad / raw vegetable salad analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a 4-topping undressed salad. GI estimated at approximately 32 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for raw mixed vegetable salad / garden salad (GI ~28–38, mean ~32). Glycemic load per 200 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (32 × 7.3) / 100 ≈ 2.3. This is consistent with the very low carbohydrate content (3.65 g/100 g) and absence of dressing.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (140 mg/100 g) and absence of sauce or dressing
hypertension.guidance: At 140 mg sodium per 100 g with no added sauce, this salad contributes a modest sodium load and is consistent with DASH dietary guidelines; patients should still monitor topping choices such as processed meats, pickled vegetables, or salted nuts that can substantially increase sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (3.65 g/100 g), low GI (~32), and meaningful dietary fibre (1.99 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content, low estimated glycemic index, and absence of dressing make this salad an excellent choice for glycaemic control in Type 2 Diabetes; patients should limit starchy toppings such as croutons, corn, or potato to preserve the low glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (0.9 g/100 g), low saturated fat, and high vegetable fibre content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The negligible fat content, absence of added oils or dressings, and vegetable-based fibre (1.99 g/100 g) support a heart-healthy dietary pattern; selecting anti-inflammatory toppings such as walnuts, flaxseeds, or oily fish would further enhance cardiovascular benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory vegetable base, very low fat, no pro-inflammatory preparation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The raw vegetable base provides antioxidants and fibre with no pro-inflammatory cooking method; individuals with RA should choose anti-inflammatory toppings (walnuts, flaxseeds, salmon) and monitor personal tolerance to nightshade vegetables such as tomato and bell pepper.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salad (4 toppings), no sauce is a customisable mixed fresh-vegetable salad served without any dressing or sauce, with a choice of four toppings. The base typically consists of mixed salad leaves, lettuce, cucumber, tomato, and carrot, assembled raw without cooking. The four toppings are variable and may include hard-boiled egg, tuna, corn, croutons, nuts, seeds, or other items depending on the establishment. The absence of sauce or dressing is the defining characteristic of this variant, eliminating added sugars, oils, and sodium that dressings typically contribute. Per 100 g, the dish provides only 31 kcal, 1.95 g protein, 0.9 g fat, and 3.65 g carbohydrate, making it one of the lowest-calorie salad options available. Dietary fibre is 1.99 g/100 g and sodium is 140 mg/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 32 (low), and the glycemic load per 200 g serving is approximately 2.3. Clinically, this salad is well-suited for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or weight. It is classified as vegetarian, plant-based, low-calorie, and broadly compatible with keto dietary patterns when starchy toppings are avoided. The dish is commonly found in Singapore hawker centres, food courts, and Western-style cafes.

How many calories in a salad with 4 toppings no sauce?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010044) at confidence 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for raw mixed vegetable salad, which is the closest defensible approved GI source for this food type.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this customisable salad preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for F08010044 as of 2026. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.15 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central data for raw mixed vegetable salad (SR Legacy), consistent with the very low total fat of 0.9 g/100 g. This food is a Western generic dish served in Singapore; multilingual local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable as this is an English-named Western dish without established local-language equivalents in common use. The dish is classified as vegetarian but not vegan because topping options may include egg or fish; is_vegan is set to false and is_gluten_free is set to false because crouton toppings may contain gluten. Topping composition varies by establishment and individual selection, which introduces inherent variability in the final nutritional profile.
